knowledge: Use `ls -a` to inventory the repo before touching files so you know which modules, patches, or scripts exist.
Run `pwd` and confirm you are inside the experiment workspace before editing paths.
Run `rg -n "keyword"` across the repo to locate definitions or uses before assuming semantics.
List Fortran sources with `rg --files -g '*.f90'` to scope the search surface.
Search for module or routine names with `rg -n "module_name" -g '*.f90'` to find declarations.
Use `rg -n "call routine_name" -g '*.f90'` to find call sites and verify control flow.
Track a config flag via `rg -n "flag_name"` to see where branches diverge.
Call `head -n 40 path/to/file` to see file headers, module names, and includes.
Use `tail -n 40` on build or run logs to spot the most recent error lines.
`wc -l file.f90` gives a quick sense of file size before deep edits.
Use `sed -n '120,200p' file.f90` or `nl -ba file.f90 | sed -n '120,200p'` to view numbered context.
Use `git diff -U3 path/to/file` to compare local edits with expected changes.
Run `git status -sb` to confirm which files changed before and after patching.
Search preprocessor guards with `rg -n "#if|#ifdef" -g '*.[Ff]90'` to check compile-time branches.
Find constants with `rg -n "parameter" -g '*.f90'` to locate default values.
Use a short Python regex to list subroutine/module names so you can map the file structure quickly.
Write a Python snippet to scan for assignments to a target variable and print line numbers.
Use Python to extract and print a specific block (between two markers) when the file is large.
Use Python to scan `call` statements across files and build a quick caller list for a routine.
Use Python's difflib to compare two versions of a source file and isolate the minimal delta.
Search namelist or input parameters with `rg -n "&" -g '*.nml'` or `rg -n "namelist"` to trace runtime flags.
List relevant configs with `rg --files -g '*.nml' -g '*.in'` so you know where inputs live.
Use `rg -n "TODO|FIXME"` to see if there are hints about known issues.
Use Python to count how often a symbol appears per file to prioritize where to inspect first.
Compute `md5sum path/to/file` before and after edits to confirm you are modifying the intended file.
